title: Cirrus: Multi-arch build auto-update + tagging update
body:
This new VM image brings in two important updates to multi-arch
image build operations:

1. Future operational changes will no-longer require also updating VM
   images.  Updates to build-push made in `containers/automation_images`
   will automatically be picked up at runtime.
   ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/123

2. On the next run, both `vX` and `vX.Y` tagged manifest-lists will be
   pushed.  This is now also reflected in the README.
   ref: https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/125

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: ***Note***: I manually triggered `Build multi-arch contrib/podmanimage/stable` ([log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4597717035909120)) and `Build multi-arch contrib/hello` ([log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5723616942751744)), and it seems I made a woopsie.  I think I can fix it right in c/automation-images w/o rebuilding...
Ugh...nope, we need https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/127 and new images because I cannot auto-update the auto-update script itself.  That part has to be inside a VM image...

...great, auto-update is working now and [properly tagging the stable image](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5685612421316608?logs=main#L3027-L3024):

```
...cut...
############################################################
Executing mod-command:  tag_version.sh
############################################################
Found image command version 'v3.4.4'
Successfully tagged quay.io/containers/podman:v3.4.4
Successfully tagged quay.io/containers/podman:v3.4
Successfully tagged quay.io/containers/podman:v3
...cut...
############################################################
Executing mod-command:  tag_version.sh
############################################################
Found image command version 'v3.4.4'
Successfully tagged quay.io/podman/stable:v3.4.4
Successfully tagged quay.io/podman/stable:v3.4
Successfully tagged quay.io/podman/stable:v3
```
(note: a "latest" tag is also pushed automatically)

The implication here is, if a `v3.4.5` came out, our images for `v3.4` and `v3` would update but we would stop building/pushing a `v3.4.4` image, so it would remain stable from that point onward.  Similar story for the other tags.
I think this is ready to go in.
